posts.text:
Whether or not a conditional pause on AI is possible in practice versus just theory, this is a significant statement from @demishassabis that the world should take into account.


Emily Chang (@emilychangtv)

One of the most interesting parts of my convo w/ @demishassabis: He would support a “pause” on AI if he knew all companies + countries would do it — so society and regulation could catch up

Video
  





— https://nitter.net/emilychangtv/status/2013726877706313798#m

posts.text:
This week, I sat down with experts @selinaxuxinyue and @mattsheehan88 on our podcast to talk about the state of AI in China. 

The AI arms race is driven by a fear that China will beat the US to an imagined finish line of AGI. But are we really racing towards the same finish line?

I highly recommend this conversation for anyone who wants to better understand how the AI race is viewed in China compared to the US, and whether international negotiation on AI is still possible (spoiler: it is).

📺

posts.text:
There's one important note I see missing: there's a a big imbalance btwn the upsides vs. the downsides of AI. 

The upsides of AI (longevity, cancer drugs, climate solutions, fusion energy, etc) don't *prevent* the downsides. But some downsides, if they happen (bio weapon, cyber catastrophes, WW3 with autonomous weapons, unemployment levels driving political revolution, loss of control) would undermine the upsides. 

Put differently, we will only get the upsides if we successfully mitigate and a

posts.text:
Important for Ai policy leaders and decision-makers understand the recent evidence of Ai models demonstrating self-awareness of when they’re being evaluated and adjusting their behavior. 

“Safety” is a mirage when Ai models recognize when they’re being watched.


Apollo Research (@apolloaievals)

When running evaluations of frontier AIs by OpenAI, Google, xAI and Anthropic for deception and other types of covert behavior, we find them increasingly frequently realizing when they are being evalua
